title: StatusMessage Object
description: Defines the message associated with a Status object.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: childStatusMessages
properties.description: Returns the collection of status codes that are children of this status message.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: message
properties.description: The user visible message being used. Setting this message for custom feature errors or warnings is currently ignored.

properties.name: messageId
properties.description: Gets and sets the ID of the message being used. This is a predefined ID within the Fusion message string table.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: statusMessageType
properties.description: Returns the type of message this StatusMessage represents.

childStatusMessages.title: StatusMessage.childStatusMessages Property
childStatusMessages.description: Returns the collection of status codes that are children of this status message.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = statusMessage_var.childStatusMessages

isValid.title: StatusMessage.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = statusMessage_var.isValid

message.title: StatusMessage.message Property
message.description: The user visible message being used. Setting this message for custom feature errors or warnings is currently ignored.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = statusMessage_var.message

# Set the value of the property.
statusMessage_var.message = propertyValue

messageId.title: StatusMessage.messageId Property
messageId.description: Gets and sets the ID of the message being used. This is a predefined ID within the Fusion message string table.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = statusMessage_var.messageId

# Set the value of the property.
statusMessage_var.messageId = propertyValue

objectType.title: StatusMessage.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = statusMessage_var.objectType

statusMessageType.title: StatusMessage.statusMessageType Property
statusMessageType.description: Returns the type of message this StatusMessage represents.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = statusMessage_var.statusMessageType

classType.title: StatusMessage.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.
